title: References to Sealed Indictments
summary: Drop #16 referenced sealed indictments and implied that major legal actions were pending. Sealed indictment claims became a recurring Q narrative.
raw_text:
Why are there so many sealed indictments?
Why is this relevant?
Who is being protected?
Who is being targeted?
Q

sources.label: U.S. Courts
sources.note: Federal court procedures and sealed filings

lines.text: Why are there so many sealed indictments?
lines.fact: Federal courts routinely issue sealed indictments for operational and legal reasons. Viral claims that unusually high indictment counts proved mass arrests were not substantiated by official evidence.
lines.theory: Q followers believed sealed indictments were connected to future arrests of political elites.
lines.context: Sealed filings are a normal part of the judicial system and are not inherently evidence of conspiracy investigations.
lines.questions: What baseline number of sealed indictments is typical in federal courts? Did public data support claims of unprecedented activity?
lines.Deltas: 2018-2025: Claims about sealed indictments continued circulating online without confirmation of mass coordinated arrests.
